source: Press Information Bureau (PIB) — Government of India — March 2026
headline: India's crude oil import dependency reaches 88.6%, government assures adequate reserves
body: India's crude oil import dependency stands at approximately 88.6% as of early 2026. The government maintains total reserve capacity of approximately 74 days, with current stock cover at around 60 days including crude oil, petroleum products, and strategic reserves. The strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) alone provides about 9.5 days of net oil imports. Approximately 70% of India's crude imports now bypass the Strait of Hormuz due to diversification to ~40 countries, though 40% still pass through the Strait.
